name: Kubernetes Fundamentals
tagline: Go from zero to deploying production-grade Kubernetes workloads
description:
Kubernetes Fundamentals is a hands-on course covering everything you need to understand and operate Kubernetes in production. You will learn the core building blocks — Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments — and progressively work up to networking with Services and Ingress, stateful workloads with StatefulSets, cluster configuration with ConfigMaps and Scheduler, and access control with RBAC.

Every topic includes both conceptual units (theory + quizzes) and coding exercises where you apply manifests against a real Kubernetes cluster. You will debug real failures, observe self-healing behavior, and build production-ready patterns.

By the end of this course you will be comfortable reading and writing Kubernetes manifests, understanding what happens inside the cluster, and troubleshooting common issues.
prerequisites: Basic Linux command line (navigating directories, running commands)
Docker fundamentals (images, containers, basic docker run)
what_you_learn: Understand Kubernetes architecture and control plane components
Deploy and manage stateless workloads with Pods and Deployments
Scale applications and observe self-healing with ReplicaSets
Expose workloads internally and externally with Services
Route HTTP traffic with Ingress controllers
Control scheduling with node affinity and taints/tolerations
Secure the cluster with RBAC roles and service accounts

author.name: KubePlayground Team
author.bio: Platform engineers with extensive production Kubernetes experience across cloud and on-premise environments. We built KubePlayground to provide realistic, hands-on K8s practice without the setup overhead.
